summary: American playwright. Best known for The Little Foxes (Regina Giddens, the Hubbard family, cotton mill, stolen bonds) and The Children's Hour (Mary Tilford accuses teachers Karen and Martha of being lesbians). Also wrote Toys in the Attic, The Autumn Garden, and Another Part of the Forest. Partner of Dashiell Hammett. Refused to name names to HUAC. Feud with Mary McCarthy.

works.name: The Little Foxes
works.description: Play (1939) about the Hubbard family in post-Reconstruction Alabama. Regina Giddens schemes against her brothers to control a cotton mill.

clues.clue: Regina blackmails brothers Ben and Oscar Hubbard over stolen Union Pacific bonds — Leo steals from wheelchair-bound Horace's safe deposit box
examples: Leo steals the wheelchair-bound Horace's Union Pacific bonds
Regina ends up blackmailing her brothers Ben and Oscar Hubbard

clues.clue: Horace dies of heart attack while crawling upstairs for medicine — Regina watches and does nothing
examples: a wheelchair-bound man dies while crawling upstairs to get his heart medication
makes no effort to help her husband as he suffers a heart attack

clues.clue: Cotton mill — brothers plan to build cotton mill with $88,000; need Chicago businessman Mr. Marshall's investment
examples: a cotton mill funded by $88,000 stolen from Horace Giddens
desires to build a cotton mill

clues.clue: Servant Addie calls Hubbards "people who ate the earth" like "the locusts" in the Bible
examples: are called 'people who eat the earth' by the servant Addie
the Hubbard family does this, 'like in the Bible with the locusts'

clues.clue: Daughter Alexandra refuses to go to Chicago with Regina — promises not to stand around and watch
examples: Alexandra Hubbard refuses to go to Chicago with her mother
her daughter accuses her of being one of the 'people who ate the earth'

clues.clue: Title from Song of Solomon — "little foxes" who "ruin the vineyards"
examples: compares the Hubbard family to animals who 'ruin the vineyards' in this biblical book

clues.clue: William Wyler directed film version
examples: William Wyler directed a film version

works.name: The Children's Hour
works.description: Play (1934) about two schoolteachers falsely accused of being lesbians by a student. Controversially lost 1935 Pulitzer to Zoe Akins's The Old Maid.

clues.clue: Mary Tilford accuses teachers Karen Wright and Martha Dobie of having a lesbian affair — blackmails Rosalie Wells into corroborating
examples: Mary Tilford claims that her teachers Karen Wright and Martha Dobie are having an affair
blackmails Rosalie Wells into corroborating her story by threatening to reveal her theft of a bracelet

clues.clue: Martha commits suicide after the accusations — Karen tells Martha "I loved you the way they said"
examples: Martha commits suicide after Mary Tilford accuses her of having an affair
one of those characters is told by the other that 'I loved you the way they said'

clues.clue: Karen leaves fiance Joe Cardin after accusations — Joe questions whether Mary is faking an illness
examples: Karen leaves her fiance Joe after Mary Tilford's false accusations
a character who fakes an illness is examined by her uncle, Joe Cardin

clues.clue: Mrs. Mortar (former actress, mentored by Sir Henry) teaches "Quality of Mercy" from Merchant of Venice
examples: a girl reading from The Merchant of Venice, a vain and aging actress reproaches her niece
Mrs. Mortar teaches a student the 'Quality of Mercy' speech

clues.clue: Amelia Tilford arrives too late to stop Martha's suicide after discovering Mary's lies
examples: Amelia Tilford arrives too late to stop the suicide

clues.clue: William Wyler directed two film versions — second paired Shirley MacLaine and Audrey Hepburn; first excised the lesbian element
examples: Wyler directed two different versions of a play by an author with this surname, with the latter pairing Shirley MacClaine and Audrey Hepburn

works.name: Toys in the Attic
works.description: Play about Julian Berniers bringing his new bride Lily home to doting spinster sisters Anna and Carrie in New Orleans.

clues.clue: Julian brings bride Lily home to spinster sisters Anna and Carrie — finances their trip to Europe with money from a real estate scam
examples: Julian brings his new bride Lily home to his doting spinster sisters
aunts Jenny and Hannah inspired the characters Anna and Carrie

clues.clue: Anna and Carrie unexpectedly reject money offered by returning brother Julian
examples: Anna and Carrie, who unexpectedly reject money offered by their returning brother Julian

clues.clue: Albertine's relationship with black chauffeur Henry — Henry told "you win many battles"
examples: a black man is told 'you win many battles' after accompanying his lover on a visit to her in-laws wearing a summer suit but holding a chauffeur's cap
Albertine enters into a relationship with a black chauffeur named Henry

works.name: The Autumn Garden
works.description: Play set at Constance Tuckerman's summer boarding house. Nick Denery passes out drunk in Sophie's bed.

clues.clue: Sophie blackmails Nina for $5,000 after Nick Denery passes out drunk in her bed — set at Constance Tuckerman's boarding house
examples: Sophie blackmails Nina for five thousand dollars after Nina's drunk husband Nick Denery passes out in her bed
set at the boarding house of Constance Tuckerman

clues.clue: General Griggs laments that he's "frittered himself away" — monologue on futility of waiting for "that big hour of decision"
examples: General Griggs laments that he's 'frittered [himself] away'

clues.clue: Ends with "Most of us lie to ourselves, darling, most of us"
examples: ends with the line 'Most of us lie to ourselves, darling, most of us'

clues.clue: Collaborated with partner Dashiell Hammett on the play
examples: collaborated with her partner Dashiell Hammett on The Autumn Garden

works.name: Another Part of the Forest
works.description: Prequel to The Little Foxes. Set in 1880; depicts the Hubbard family under patriarch Marcus.

clues.clue: Prequel to The Little Foxes — Lavinia wishes to start school for Black children; emotionally abused by husband Marcus
examples: Lavinia wishes to start a school for Black children and is emotionally abused by her husband Marcus
this prequel play to The Little Foxes

clues.clue: Marcus led Union troops to a massacre of Confederate soldiers — has wife declared insane to keep her quiet
examples: has his wife declared insane to discredit her if she reveals his 'sin,' the time he led Union troops to a massacre of Confederate soldiers

clues.clue: Regina attempts to escape Marcus's tyranny by marrying Confederate officer John Bagtry
examples: Regina attempts to escape the tyranny of her father by marrying the former Confederate officer John Bagtry

clues.clue: Refused to name names to HUAC — "I cannot and will not cut my conscience to fit this year's fashions"
examples: I cannot and will not cut my conscience to fit this year's fashions
refused to answer questions about other people in a letter to HUAC

clues.clue: Feud with Mary McCarthy — McCarthy said "every word she writes is a lie, including 'and' and 'the'" on Dick Cavett Show; Hellman sued for libel
examples: every word this author writes is a lie, including 'and' and 'the'
initiated a defamation suit after being accused of doing this by a guest on The Dick Cavett Show

clues.clue: Partner of Dashiell Hammett
examples: the wife of Dashiell Hammett
collaborated with her partner Dashiell Hammett

clues.clue: Memoir Pentimento — "Julia" chapter about anti-Nazi smuggling ($50,000 in fur hat); Muriel Gardiner claimed she was the real Julia

clues.clue: Feud fictionalized in Nora Ephron's play Imaginary Friends — Hellman and McCarthy recall feud in hell
examples: Nora Ephron's play Imaginary Friends, which depicts a scandal

clues.clue: Wrote original libretto for Bernstein's Candide
examples: wrote the original libretto for Leonard Bernstein's Candide

clues.clue: Wrote controversial screenplay The North Star (Ukrainian partisans vs. Nazis)
examples: wrote a wartime film about Ukrainian guerillas that was re-edited in the 50s to reference the Hungarian Revolution

clues.clue: Declined to adapt The Diary of Anne Frank; suggested Frances Goodrich and Albert Hackett instead
examples: When asked to adapt The Diary of Anne Frank for the stage, this playwright declined

comprehensive_summary:
Lillian Hellman is an American playwright best known for The Little Foxes and The Children's Hour. The Little Foxes (1939) depicts the scheming Hubbard family in post-Reconstruction Alabama, centered on Regina Giddens who blackmails her brothers Ben and Oscar after nephew Leo steals Union Pacific bonds from Regina's wheelchair-bound husband Horace's safe deposit box. Horace dies of a heart attack while crawling upstairs for medicine as Regina watches without helping. The servant Addie calls the Hubbards "people who ate the earth" like "the locusts" in the Bible. Daughter Alexandra ultimately refuses to accompany Regina to Chicago. The title comes from the Song of Solomon.

The Children's Hour (1934) centers on student Mary Tilford's false accusation that her teachers Karen Wright and Martha Dobie are having a lesbian affair. Mary blackmails Rosalie Wells into corroborating her story. Martha eventually commits suicide, and Karen tells her "I loved you the way they said." Karen also breaks off her engagement to Joe Cardin. The play controversially lost the 1935 Pulitzer to Zoe Akins's The Old Maid.

Toys in the Attic depicts Julian Berniers bringing his new bride Lily home to his doting spinster sisters Anna and Carrie in New Orleans. Albertine has a relationship with her black chauffeur Henry. The Autumn Garden is set at Constance Tuckerman's summer boarding house, where Sophie blackmails Nina for $5,000 after her drunk husband Nick Denery passes out in Sophie's bed. Another Part of the Forest is a prequel to The Little Foxes depicting the Hubbard family under patriarch Marcus.

Hellman refused to name names to HUAC, declaring "I cannot and will not cut my conscience to fit this year's fashions." Her feud with Mary McCarthy became legendary when McCarthy said on The Dick Cavett Show that "every word she writes is a lie, including 'and' and 'the'," prompting Hellman to sue for libel. Her memoir Pentimento, especially the "Julia" chapter about smuggling $50,000 in a fur hat to fund the Austrian resistance, was disputed by Muriel Gardiner. She was the partner of Dashiell Hammett and wrote the original libretto for Bernstein's Candide.
